entry.title: PQC: Lattice-based signatures
entry.summary:
The discussion emphasizes the significant role of lattices in the domain of post-quantum cryptography. The preference for lattice-based solutions is evidenced by their prominence among the finalists in a notable competition held by NIST, where three out of four top contenders employ this method. This strong representation highlights the broad consensus on the efficacy of lattices in ensuring cryptographic security in a future quantum computing paradigm. Lattice-based methods are particularly valued for their robust security proofs and the ability to establish security from worst-case scenarios to more common average-case conditions.
On the other hand, while hash-based cryptographic methods continue to be relevant, especially considering their application in systems like Bitcoin that rely on established security assumptions, they were positioned as secondary options in the NIST event. This suggests a strategic placement where hash-based methods could serve as fallbacks rather than primary solutions in the evolving landscape of cryptography. This nuanced approach in selecting cryptographic methods underscores the complexity and depth of considerations necessary when transitioning to post-quantum cryptography, where both theoretical and practical aspects must be intricately balanced.
